page: Amazon Web Services r8idn.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel processors running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, delivering high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. For network-heavy workloads, the r8idn.12xlarge offers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps and is optimized for both network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) performance. It does not include any GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local NVMe storage, the r8idn.12xlarge offers resource density suitable for memory-intensive applications. The combination of dedicated Intel processing power, high baseline network throughput, and local NVMe SSDs makes this instance type well-suited for large-scale databases, real-time analytics, in-memory caches, and enterprise applications requiring high-throughput data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idn.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel processors running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM. The instance is equipped with 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps, optimized for both network and EBS performance. With no integrated GPUs, this instance is designed for high-throughput, memory-intensive workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, while the Nitro hypervisor provides secure and efficient virtualization. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage make it highly efficient for hosting large databases, in-memory caches, and real-time data analytics pipelines.
